title: Kernel lane / compatibility critique. Review two candidate changes conceptually in /Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration: 1) making run-linked-systems propagate blocked/error exit codes upstream, 2) making backup scan lock contention visible instead of silent success. Identify compatibility risks...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: `blocked` vs `error` semantics are not fully propagated to consumer repos. The dispatch payload still sends only `success_count`/`error_count` and omits `blocked_count`, so retryable lock/contention states get flattened downstream ([run-linked-systems.sh:304](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-linked-systems.sh:304), [fugue-result-receiver.yml:27](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/.github/workflows/templates/fugue-result-receiver.yml:27)). 2. Medium: Upstream behavior changes from “report-and-continue” to fail-fast when linked systems are enabled; `run-local-orchestration` now exits with linked runner RC, and callers like `gha24` hard-exit on any non-zero. This is a compatibility break for flows that used linked systems as non-blocking observability ([run-local-orchestration.sh:1045](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh:1045), [run-local-orchestration.sh:1374](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh:1374), [gha24:562](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/gha24:562)). 3. Medium: Backup scan lock contention is now visible (`75` + `BLOCKED lock_busy`), which is correct contract-wise but will increase recurring “failure-like” signals under launchd overlap (default 30s interval). Any monitoring/supervision expecting silent `0` must be adjusted ([run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:209](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:209), [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh:10](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh:10), [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh:83](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh:83)). 4. Medium: CI/gate coverage does not clearly enforce the new linked/backup exit-contract tests in the main orchestration gate list, so regressions can slip unless local hook paths are used ([fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:240](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:240), [check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:367](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:367)). **Tests That Must Be Updated** 1. `tests/test-linked-systems-exit-contract.sh`: keep/extend assertions for adapter `75 -> blocked` and `non-75 -> error (70)` plus integrated counters ([test-linked-systems-exit-contract.sh:86](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-linked-systems-exit-contract.sh:86)). 2. `tests/test-local-orchestration-gates.sh`: assert parent propagation of linked blocked/error RCs and `linked_systems_exit_code` in integrated output ([test-local-orchestration-gates.sh:467](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-local-orchestration-gates.sh:467)). 3. `tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh`: assert scan-mode lock contention returns `75` with explicit reason text (not silent success), while explicit record under scan lock still queues/degrades as designed ([test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:238](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:238), [test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:263](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:263)). 4. Gate wiring: ensure the above tests are invoked in the default CI/readiness path, not only file-touch hooks. No files were edited.
